# W22 Probe J — "do nothing" baseline (reuse prev AMFC)

*Auto-generated by `experiments/analyze_probe_j.py`.*

## Sample

- Records: 1839
- Scenarios: 2
- Seeds: 5
- Periods: 23
- Mean L1 dist to closest-match portfolio: 1.3041

## Verdict

🟢 **AMFC beats "do nothing"** in 83.2% of transitions — optimization is justified

## Realized HV comparison

| DM | n periods | mean realized HV | std |
|---|---|---|---|
| amfc | 220 | 1.7283e-04 | 3.3893e-04 |
| do_nothing | 220 | 1.4407e-04 | 2.9715e-04 |

- Δ = AMFC − DoNothing = +2.8762e-05 (+19.96%)

## Per-scenario breakdown

| scenario | n | AMFC mean | DoNothing mean | Fraction DN better |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| ASMS_mHDM_K3_v2both | 110 | 1.8315e-04 | 1.5285e-04 | 0.1727 |
| SMS_RDM_K0 | 110 | 1.6251e-04 | 1.3529e-04 | 0.1636 |

## Caveat

- "Do nothing" baseline is APPROXIMATE: it uses the period-t Pareto-front portfolio closest to period-(t-1)'s AMFC (mean L1 dist = 1.3041, max possible = 2.0).
- True "buy and hold" would require re-evaluating period-(t-1)'s AMFC weights with period-t's actual asset returns — not directly possible from the probe log (no asset-level OOS returns logged). The full Probe J would require adding asset-level OOS data logging to walk_forward.py.
- This approximation BIASES the do-nothing baseline downward when the closest match has noticeable L1 distance, so the true do-nothing value may be HIGHER (more competitive against AMFC).
